summary: Matched the common fighter Wait action callbacks by moving `ftCo_Wait_Anim`, `ftCo_Wait_IASA`, `ftCo_Wait_Phys`, and `ftCo_Wait_Coll` out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s` into a new C file under `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Wait.c`. The PR also split the surrounding assembly into a new `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, added headers for the new C and remaining asm helpers, adjusted `obj_files.mk` ordering, and refined `ftData` layout enough to expose `ft_data->x24` used by Wait animation logic. There were no review comments or body text in the provided slice, so intent beyond matching `ftCo_Wait` is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Wait.c` implementing the four Wait callbacks: `ftCo_Wait_Anim`, `ftCo_Wait_IASA`, `ftCo_Wait_Phys`, and `ftCo_Wait_Coll`.
`ftCo_Wait_Anim` now uses `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`, checks `fp->x2224_flag.bits.b2`, calls `ft_800C8B74()` for that flagged path, otherwise calls `ft_8008A7A8(gobj, fp->ft_data->x24)`.

`ftCo_Wait_IASA` was expressed as a short-circuit chain of negated transition/input checks including `ft_80096540`, multiple `ft_800D*` and `ft_8008*` helpers, `ftFx_AppealS_CheckInput`, `ft_800DE9D8`, `ft_800D5FB0`, and others, returning only when no check succeeds.
`ftCo_Wait_Phys` was matched as sequential calls to `ft_80084F3C(gobj)` and `ftColl_8007AEE0(gobj)`.
`ftCo_Wait_Coll` was matched as a direct call to `ft_80084280(gobj)`.
Added `ftCo_Wait.h` with prototypes and address comments for the four callbacks, using an address-derived guard `GALE01_08A494`.
Removed the Wait callback declarations from `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h`, moving them to the dedicated ftCommon header.
Added `src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h` for remaining helpers used by the new C, including `ft_8008A7A8`, `ft_800C8B74`, `ft_800D5FB0`, and `ft_800DE9D8`.

Updated `obj_files.mk` to place `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Wait.c.o` after `asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s.o` and before the new `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o`, preserving the original text layout while replacing only the matched Wait region.
Split the original large `ft_0877.s` assembly: the matched Wait functions were removed and the subsequent assembly was added as `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Adjusted `src/melee/ft/types.h` layout annotations for `ftData` and replaced part of the previous filler with explicit fields including `/* +24 */ int x24`, which is used by `ftCo_Wait_Anim`.
Added includes of `ftCo_Wait.h` in `ftCo_Init.c` and `ftDk_HeavyJump.c` so users of the moved prototypes reference the dedicated header.
smart_moves: Limited the C match to the small, high-confidence Wait callback quartet while moving the remaining unmatched assembly into a follow-on `ft_08A4.s` object instead of attempting an oversized conversion.
Preserved object ordering in `obj_files.mk`, which is critical for matching when replacing a small range in the middle of a large assembly translation unit.
Created a dedicated `ftCo_Wait.h` rather than leaving common-action declarations in the broad `ft_0877.h`, improving include locality for future ftCommon decomp work.
Exposed only the needed `ftData` member `x24` with offset comments instead of over-naming or over-typing the entire struct.
Used a single C short-circuit expression for the long IASA transition chain, matching the assembly pattern where many checks branch to the same exit label if any succeeds.
Added `.global` labels in the remaining assembly around split boundaries, which suggests care was taken to keep cross-object branch/call references resolvable after the asm split.
decomp_lessons: When matching a function embedded in a large asm file, split the asm at the matched function boundary and add the new C object in exact link order; this allows incremental matching without disturbing later code addresses.
For common action callbacks, keep the canonical quartet naming pattern `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`, and put the prototypes in an action-specific ftCommon header.
A long sequence of boolean transition checks can often match as a chained `&&` of negated calls when the original assembly exits on the first successful transition.
Struct layout improvements can be minimal and evidence-driven: exposing `ftData.x24` was enough to express the Wait animation path while retaining unknown ranges as `u8` filler.
Header cleanup matters during matching: moving declarations out of broad numeric headers helps subsequent files include the semantic action header and reduces dependency sprawl.
If a split assembly file still needs local labels or branch targets across object boundaries, label visibility may need to be adjusted with `.global` entries.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_Wait_*` is used for common fighter Wait action callbacks.
The common action callback suffixes are `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
Dedicated action header name mirrors the C file: `ftCo_Wait.c` and `ftCo_Wait.h`.
Address comments in headers preserve symbol provenance, e.g. `/* 08A494 */ void ftCo_Wait_Anim(...)`.
Unmatched helper declarations remain numeric-address style, e.g. `ft_8008A7A8`, `ft_800C8B74`, `ft_800D5FB0`, `ft_800DE9D8`.
The new asm/header split uses the address boundary naming pattern `ft_08A4.s` and `ft_08A4.h`.
The `ftData` struct kept offset-style member names such as `x24` where semantic names were not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the exact assembly range beginning at `ftCo_Wait_Anim` with C and moved subsequent unmatched assembly into `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Maintained link order by listing `ft_0877.s.o`, then `ftCo_Wait.c.o`, then `ft_08A4.s.o` in `obj_files.mk`.
Matched `ftCo_Wait_IASA` with a chained short-circuit conditional; this mirrors repeated `cmpwi`/branch checks converging on a shared function epilogue.
Matched the Wait animation path by recognizing the fighter flag bit access `fp->x2224_flag.bits.b2` and the `ft_data->x24` argument to `ft_8008A7A8`.
Added global labels such as `.L_800886D8`, `.L_80089118`, and `.L_8008A244` in the assembly diff, likely to support symbol resolution after splitting or after calls were renamed.
Renamed at least one assembly call target from a local label form `.L_800C8B74` to the function symbol `ft_800C8B74`, aligning the assembly reference with the new C prototype.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further decompile `ft_8008A7A8` and the remaining `ft_08A4.s` functions.
Look for PRs that give semantic names to `ftData.x24` or refine the surrounding `ftData` layout.
Search for later cleanup of `ft_800C8B74`, `ft_800D5FB0`, and `ft_800DE9D8` declarations after this temporary `ft_08A4.h` header.
Compare other ftCommon action matches that use the same `Anim`/`IASA`/`Phys`/`Coll` quartet pattern and object-order split tactic.
